caption:
5 React hooks you probably never used.

React 19 shipped hooks that solve problems you've been hacking around for years.

Stop writing workarounds. Use the built-in solution.

📌 SAVE this — you'll need at least 2 of these this week.

Which one surprised you? Comment the number 👇

#react #react19 #hooks #javascript #typescript #webdev #coding #programming #frontend #nextjs #codingtips

slides.title: React hooks you never used
slides.subtitle: Built into React 19. No libraries needed.

slides.title: useId( ) — unique IDs, SSR-safe
slides.desc: Generates stable IDs that survive server rendering. No hydration mismatch. No Math.random hacks.

slides.title: useOptimistic( ) — instant UI
slides.desc: Show the result before the server confirms. Auto-rolls back on failure.
slides.punchline: Zero perceived latency. Server catches up.

slides.title: useFormStatus( ) — pending for free
slides.desc: Know if a parent form is submitting. No props. No context. Just call it.
slides.punchline: Works inside any form. Zero prop drilling.

slides.title: useDeferredValue( ) — smooth typing
slides.desc: Deprioritize expensive re-renders so the UI stays responsive during fast input.
slides.punchline: Type fast. No jank. React handles the rest.

slides.title: useSyncExternalStore( )
slides.desc: Subscribe to anything outside React — localStorage, WebSocket, Redux — without tearing.
slides.punchline: The bridge between React and everything else.

slides.title: All 5 hooks decoded

items.label: useFormStatus()
items.desc: Is the form submitting? No props needed

slides.question: Which one are you using first? 👇

slides.teaser: Next: the one CSS trick that replaced 50 lines of JavaScript
